claim_boundary: Exact-row, same-host, bounded decode-at-depth snapshot for Llama 3.2 3B Instruct Q8_0 on one Apple M4 (16GB): 64 greedy tokens after ~1.5k-token and ~8k-token prompts, three same-session rounds with alternating runtime order. Camelid reads BELOW both comparators at depth (median 25.05 vs llama.cpp 26.43 / MLX-LM 26.93 at ~1.5k; 16.94 vs 19.14 / 22.23 at ~8k). This bundle records a known-behind lane; no win is claimed. Prompt token counts differ slightly per tokenizer.

workload.model_row: Llama 3.2 3B Instruct Q8_0 (GGUF for camelid and llama.cpp; mlx-community 8-bit for MLX-LM)
workload.depths: ~1500-token and ~8012-token prompts (same text files for all runtimes), 64 greedy tokens
workload.mlx_command: mlx_lm.generate verbose, 64 tokens after the same prompt files

notes: Pre-split-K (commit 595b6e4) the same camelid probes read ~16 and ~6 tok/s. The remaining 8k gap tracks KV-cache element width: camelid streams f32 K/V (no split-K kv16 variant yet) while comparators stream half-width caches.
